items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument artwork agreement finalized for Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust regarding the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. This public art installation adds a named memorial piece to one of the city's major parks.

items.headline: City Council Appoints New Permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This is a significant leadership decision for the city's operations and administration.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates urban water master plan and drought contingency strategy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed the draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These documents outline how the city will manage water supply and respond to potential shortages, directly affecting residential water availability and conservation policies.

items.headline: Spring community grants and neighborhood event funding decisions announced
items.summary: The City Council considered applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program spring cycle on May 18. This program funds local events and neighborhood initiatives that residents may benefit from or want to participate in.

items.headline: City develops principles to help residents switch from gas to electric
items.summary: Palo Alto is creating electrification programs and principles to help residents transition away from natural gas appliances in compliance with regional air quality regulations. The council heard a status update on residential electrification efforts.

items.headline: City updates rules for backyard dwelling units to match new state law
items.summary: Palo Alto is amending its municipal code to align accessory and junior accessory dwelling unit regulations with recent changes in California state law. The ordinance updates chapters in the zoning and development code.

items.headline: Stream corridor protection rules under review
items.summary: The City Council is considering amendments to stream corridor protection standards in the municipal code. The details of what protections are being modified were not provided in the agenda.

items.headline: Auto sales use proposed for El Camino Real commercial lot
items.summary: Planning Commission scheduled to review a special development permit allowing auto sales at 975 W. El Camino Real. The existing commercial building would not expand or change its exterior; changes would be limited to parking lot repaving, restriping, and landscaping upgrades.
